titleStmt.title: Letter from E O'Sullivan to Ellen Daly, 6 April 1923
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: E O'Sullivan

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Mrs C W. Daly
Knockane
Firies,
CO Kerry
Ireland
184, Avenue Victor Hugo
Paris 6/4/23 Dear Mrs Daly I have put
off writing to you every
day since I heard of poor
Charlie's noble death. I
know what such a news
must have been to you, Con
and every member of the
family. May God give you
III He had one of those natures
to be beloved by all. Now, that he had done
his utmost for the cause
God took him to himself.
He was not any longer
for this world. R.I.P
He died a holy, and I'm
certain a happy death. You must pray & trust
in God, You have a
large family to live for.
4 I hope Bill & Tom are
alright & please G. you will
have them again when things
will quiten down. I also
trust Susie's cold is better. For my part I felt Charlies
death awfully, at first
Josie & myself were hoping
it may be a false report. I will now close with
my most sincere wishes to
you all that God may give
you the grace to bear your trial Yours sic
E O'Sullivan P.S. Tell May I often think
of her
